ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general waffle entry (FOODON_00002765); no specific peanut butter waffle entry exists. Closest parent match used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A00LC covers waffles generically; no peanut butter variant code exists. Closest parent match used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for peanut butter waffle; left null to maintain ontology honesty.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard peanut butter waffle weighing approximately 75 g, as commonly served as a snack or light meal.

nutrient_source.title: Waffle (peanut butter) — Singapore HPB Foodo Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Waffle (peanut butter), cr_id F11100032, with confidence 0.95. All per-100g macros and micronutrients are taken from this source.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for peanut butter waffle in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI estimated at 76 using plain waffle as the closest analogous food (GI ~76, per Atkinson et al. 2008, Diabetes Care). Peanut butter filling adds fat and protein which may modestly lower the effective GI in practice; however, the plain waffle GI is used as a conservative upper-bound estimate. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (76 × 44.28 × 0.75) / 100 = 25.2 g per 75 g serving.

hypertension.key_factor: Moderate sodium (179 mg/100g) combined with high sugar (16.0 g/100g) and low dietary fibre (2.5 g/100g)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size to one small waffle (≤75 g) and choose reduced-sodium, no-added-sugar peanut butter, as the combination of 179 mg sodium and 16 g sugar per 100 g can contribute to blood pressure elevation and insulin-mediated sodium retention.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High sugar content (16.0 g/100g) with insufficient dietary fibre (2.5 g/100g) and high estimated GI (~76)
type2_diabetes.guidance: This food is not recommended for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes due to 16.0 g sugar per 100 g, a high estimated GI of 76, and only 2.5 g dietary fibre, which together produce a high glycaemic load (~25 per serving) and rapid postprandial blood glucose elevation; if consumed, strict portion control (≤50 g) and pairing with a low-GI food is advised.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Elevated total fat (21.55 g/100g) with estimated saturated fat ~6 g/100g and high sugar (16.0 g/100g)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should consume this food only occasionally, as the estimated saturated fat of ~6 g/100g approaches the 5 g/100g threshold associated with LDL elevation, and the high sugar content (16.0 g/100g) contributes to hypertriglyceridaemia; choosing natural peanut butter without hydrogenated oils reduces trans fat risk.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Estimated saturated fat ~6 g/100g exceeds 5 g/100g pro-inflammatory threshold; high sugar (16.0 g/100g) promotes systemic inflammation
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: This food is not recommended for individuals with rheumatoid arthritis as the estimated saturated fat (~6 g/100g) exceeds the 5 g/100g threshold linked to TNF-alpha and IL-6 cytokine upregulation, and the high sugar content (16.0 g/100g) further promotes inflammatory pathways.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Waffle with Peanut Butter is a processed snack food consisting of a waffle made from pancake-style batter — typically containing wheat flour, eggs, milk, sugar, and leavening agents — filled or topped with peanut butter. The waffle is cooked in a waffle iron, producing a crisp, grid-patterned exterior with a soft interior, while the peanut butter filling contributes a rich, nutty, creamy flavour and dense mouthfeel. Per 100 g, this food provides 411 kcal, 10.07 g protein, 21.55 g fat (estimated saturated fat ~6 g), and 44.28 g carbohydrate including 16.02 g sugar and 2.5 g dietary fibre, with 179 mg sodium. The high sugar and fat content make it a calorie-dense snack. Clinically, the high sugar (16 g/100g), high estimated GI (~76), and low fibre (2.5 g/100g) make this food unsuitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes or hypertension. The estimated saturated fat content (~6 g/100g) is a concern for cardiovascular and rheumatoid arthritis patients. Peanut butter contributes beneficial monounsaturated fatty acids and protein, but as a minor ingredient in a processed waffle, its anti-inflammatory benefit is limited. This snack is commonly consumed in Singapore and internationally as a street food or packaged snack item.
common_user_queries: Can diabetics eat peanut butter waffles?
How many calories in a peanut butter waffle?
Is waffle with peanut butter suitable for hypertension?
What is the GI of a peanut butter waffle?
Is peanut butter waffle high in sugar?

confidence_reason: Core macronutrient and micronutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id F11100032) with a reported confidence of 0.95. Saturated fat estimated at ~6 g/100g based on USDA FoodData Central data for comparable peanut butter waffles (USDA SR Legacy #18369 and peanut butter reference), as HPB does not separately report saturated fat for this entry. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database using plain waffle as the reference food.
notes: 1. Saturated fat (6.0 g/100g) is an estimate derived from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy data for a comparable peanut butter waffle, as the HPB source does not separately report saturated fat for this entry. 2. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, water_g, and all vitamin fields are set to -1: detailed fatty acid profile, moisture, and vitamin data for this specific peanut butter waffle are not available in the HPB Singapore Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central for this exact formulation as of April 2026. 3. cholesterol_mg is reported as 0.0 by HPB source; this is confirmed zero per the HPB database entry. 4. GI value of 76 is an analogous estimate using plain waffle from the University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care); no direct GI measurement for peanut butter waffle exists in the approved GI database. 5. This food is classified as a Western/International snack; Singaporean multilingual naming requirements do not apply as this is not a Singaporean-origin dish. Multilingual local_names fields are left null accordingly.
